description:
You are acting as the on-call storage administrator.  
Inside your home directory there is an existing Git repository located at  

  /home/user/storage-utils  

The repository currently contains two tracked files:

1. /home/user/storage-utils/cleanup.sh – a trivial Bash script (already committed).
2. /home/user/storage-utils/VERSION – a single-line text file whose only line is the literal string “1.2.0”.

There is also a CHANGELOG.md file that already lists historical versions 1.1.0 and 1.2.0.  
Your job is to prepare the next patch release (1.2.1) while keeping to the team’s exact release-note format.

When you finish, the repository must satisfy **all** of the following assertions (they are what the automated grader will check):

A. Semantic-version bump  
   A1. The file /home/user/storage-utils/VERSION contains exactly one line and that line is the string  
       1.2.1  
       followed by a single trailing newline (no spaces).

B. Git metadata  
   B1. Your changes are committed on branch feat/disk-space-alert.  
   B2. The commit that bumps the version is the tip of that branch and its full commit message (subject line) is  
       feat: add --dry-run option, bump version to 1.2.1  
       (The grader ignores the body, but the subject must match **exactly**).  
   B3. The repository carries a lightweight tag named v1.2.1 that points to the exact same commit as in B2.

C. CHANGELOG entry  
   C1. The file /home/user/storage-utils/CHANGELOG.md starts with the following five lines (no extra blank lines above or between them):  
       ## [1.2.1] - YYYY-MM-DD  
       ### Added  
       - Support for --dry-run to report projected free space without deleting any data.  
   Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s UTC date in ISO-8601 format (e.g., 2023-05-07).  
   C2. Earlier release entries for 1.2.0 and 1.1.0 must remain unchanged and appear below the new section.

D. Script enhancement  
   D1. cleanup.sh now recognises an optional first argument “--dry-run”.  
       • If invoked as “cleanup.sh --dry-run”, it MUST print exactly one line to stdout that starts with  
         DRY-RUN:  
         followed by a single space and the number of MiB that *would* be freed (any integer is fine).  
       • When invoked without arguments it should behave as before (deleting files and printing freed MiB).  
       The grader will call both invocation forms, so make sure the script exits with status 0 in each case.

E. Operational proof log  
   E1. After performing the tasks above, run the new dry-run mode once and capture its stdout line (described in D1) into a new file  
       /home/user/storage-utils/disk_report.log  
       This log file must contain exactly that one line and a trailing newline.

Hints & Constraints  
• You are free to decide how much space the script claims it can free; any integer ≥0 is accepted.  
• Do NOT rewrite existing history; add on top of what is there.  
• Do not use sudo.  
• Keep the total number of terminal commands in the 5-10 range.

When everything is done, the five automated checks A–E must all succeed.
truth:
Initial repository state before the agent begins:

Directory /home/user/storage-utils exists.

Contents:
1. cleanup.sh  
   #!/usr/bin/env bash  
   # Simple placeholder for cleaning /tmp  
   freed=123  
   echo "Freed ${freed} MiB"

2. VERSION  
   1.2.0\n

3. CHANGELOG.md  
   ## [1.2.0] - 2023-05-01  
   ### Changed  
   - Improved file deletion speed.  
   ## [1.1.0] - 2023-04-15  
   ### Added  
   - Initial cleanup implementation.

There is one commit on main branch with message "chore: initial import 1.2.0". No tags yet.

Expected final state after agent:

Branch feat/disk-space-alert exists and is checked out.

cleanup.sh modified:

#!/usr/bin/env bash  
if [[ "$1" == "--dry-run" ]]; then  
  freed=456  
  echo "DRY-RUN: ${freed} MiB"  
  exit 0  
fi  
freed=456  
echo "Freed ${freed} MiB"

VERSION now "1.2.1\n"

CHANGELOG.md starts:

## [1.2.1] - 2024-02-10  
### Added  
- Support for --dry-run to report projected free space without deleting any data.  
## [1.2.0] - 2023-05-01  
### Changed  
- Improved file deletion speed.  
## [1.1.0] - 2023-04-15  
### Added  
- Initial cleanup implementation.

disk_report.log in repo root contains:  
DRY-RUN: 456 MiB\n

Commit message as described.

Tag v1.2.1 pointing to commit.
